commit.message:
Fix doNotDeploy bypassed when broader target precedes it in the target list (#4688)

`MatchTargetCustomizations` returns on the first matching target. If a
broader-matching customization (e.g. `clusterGroup: all`) is listed
before a `doNotDeploy: true` customization (e.g. `clusterGroup: one`),
the doNotDeploy entry is never evaluated for clusters that match the
broader entry first.

Additionally, `target.DoNotDeploy` (from the main `Match()` call) was
not being checked when no targetCustomization was found.

Add `HasDoNotDeployTarget()` to `BundleMatch`, which scans all
targets—not just the first match—for a `doNotDeploy` flag. Update
`Targets()` in `builder.go` to consult this method before building a
`Target`, and separately honour `target.DoNotDeploy` from the GitRepo
match. Add an integration test covering the first-match bypass scenario.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: jesse.mirza@nslogin.nl <jesse.mirza@ns.nl>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): capture exit code correctly in monitoring workflows

GitHub Actions uses 'set -e' by default, so when the monitor scripts
exit with code 1 (changes detected), bash aborts before EXIT_CODE=\True
can execute. This means the exit_code output is never set, and the
PR creation condition always evaluates to false.

Fix: wrap the python invocation with set +e / set -e so exit code 1
is captured properly. Applied to both learn-monitor and
regulatory-monitoring workflows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement OpenViking-inspired capabilities (hotness score, used() feedback, L0/L1/L2, fast/quality paths, memory taxonomy, tool stats)

Borrowed 5 high-value capabilities from volcengine/OpenViking, maximally
leveraging openJiuwen framework built-ins (AgenticRetriever for quality path,
rrf_fusion framework already available).

New capabilities:
- Hotness Score: sigmoid(log1p(active_count)) × exp(-λ×age_days) blended
  into RRF fusion at alpha=0.2 (context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py)
- used() feedback API: POST /context/used increments active_count on
  confirmed context items, driving Hotness Score
- MemoryCategory 6-taxonomy: profile/preferences/entities/events/cases/patterns
  with AggregationRequest.category_filter support
- ContextLevel L0/L1/L2: ABSTRACT/OVERVIEW/DETAIL levels with
  AggregationRequest.max_level progressive expansion
- fast/quality dual-path: mode='quality' activates AgenticRetriever
  (openJiuwen-native) for complex tasks; mode='fast' (default) uses
  HybridRetriever for low-latency simple queries
- Tool Performance Memory: ToolContextGovernor.record_tool_result() +
  POST /tools/result endpoint; unreliable tools rank lower in select_tools()

Files changed:
- context_agent/models/context.py: +ContextLevel, +MemoryCategory enums,
  +ContextItem.{level,category,active_count,updated_at} fields
- context_agent/core/memory/hotness.py: new hotness pure function module
- context_agent/core/retrieval/search_coordinator.py: hotness-blended RRF
- context_agent/core/memory/working_memory.py: +mark_used() method
- context_agent/core/memory/tiered_router.py: +record_usage() method
- context_agent/core/retrieval/tool_governor.py: +record_tool_result(), +_sort_by_success_rate()

Revert: remove inlineStylesheets and minify=false

@font-face from <Font /> is always inlined by Astro and cannot
be moved to an external file, so these settings had no effect
on the intended goal.

commit.message:
IR-RA: add enclosure generation and solver integration (#3641)

This PR introduces enclosure generation for real arithmetic expressions
and integrates the support into the SMT layer and supported solvers.

### Changes
- Implement real arithmetic enclosure generation
- Integrate support into SMT core layer
- Add regression tests under regression/ir-ra

This PR is isolated from formatting-only changes.

---------

- Add skill template scaffold with gitkeep placeholders
- Consolidate platform adapters into single adaptor.md files
- Remove deprecated manifest.json, tools-registry.json, README.md,
  and frontmatter/ directories from all platform adapters
- Remove deprecated platform schemas (_schemas/)
- Update normative references (structure, linting, schemas, grammar,
  error taxonomy) with CSV support and clarifications
- Replace aps-v1.1.11 agent with aps-v1.1.16 agent

commit.message:
chore: remove 35 specs migrated to codervisor/synodic

These specs have been migrated and renumbered in the synodic repo,
which handles AI-native agent orchestration separately from ClawDen's
claw-runtime management.

Migrated spec range: 053, 054, 060-092
Updated spec 057 to remove stale parent reference to 053.

See: https://github.com/codervisor/synodic

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

when the APS skill is upgraded. Store badge markup as APS_BADGE constant.

refactor: update all agents to comply with APS v1.1.16

- Remove read/listDir from 6 agents (not in platform adapter, never
  used in processes)
- Replace invalid execute/runTests with execute/runInTerminal in
  implementer agent; add TEST_COMMAND runtime variable
- Replace NOTE: lines with TELL statements in architect agent (NOTE
  is not a valid APS DSL keyword)
- Rename excali process IDs from snake_case to kebab-case per APS
  naming conventions
- Add missing APS sections to AGENTS.md (formats, runtime, triggers,
  input) for structural compliance
- Fix SET sources in AGENTS.md processes to use "Agent Inference"
  per APS DSL specification
